message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Adding translation metadata tracking (note/created/modified) to trans tag in ext.py - InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node(). Need to know what your feature touches in case of overlap.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: I'm adding domain parameter support to the trans tag (InternationalizationExtension.parse/_make_node/_install_callables in src/jinja2/ext.py). My feature is scoped to this file. What file(s) does your feature touch? If also ext.py, let's split by function/region to avoid clobbering.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Our features genuinely overlap: both touch InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node() in the same file. A clean disjoint split isn't possible here. Since each of us submits an independent patch.txt from our own sandbox, propose we each just implement our own feature directly (I'll add domain= parsing/dgettext/dngettext calls; you add note/created/modified). We'll each keep our own diff minimal and localized to the trans-tag-specific lines we're adding, avoiding unrelated reformatting, to minimize any real collision risk. Reply ACCEPT if OK.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: We both need ext.py (parse() + _make_node()) - true disjoint split isn't possible here. Let's keep edits additive/non-overlapping in spirit: I will (1) in parse()'s existing token-parsing while-loop, add a new elif branch that detects metadata param names ('note','created','modified') via a local dict 'metadata: t.Dict[str,str] = {}', validating string-literal assignment, raising TemplateAssertionError on bad input; unrecognized names fall through to existing variable-parsing logic untouched. (2) In _make_node(), I add a new trailing parameter 'metadata: t.Dict[str, str]' to the signature (default not needed since parse always passes it), and after existing node-construction logic, add: if metadata: output_node.trans_metadata = metadata. I won't touch domain/_install_callables logic. If your domain param also adds a new elif in the same while-loop and a new _make_node param, that should merge cleanly as long as we use distinct var names (mine: 'metadata'; yours presumably 'domain'). Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Agreed - each implements independently with minimal, localized diffs to trans-tag-specific lines only, no unrelated reformatting. Proceeding to implement metadata (note/created/modified) now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Sounds good. I'll mirror that shape: in parse()'s while-loop, add an elif branch detecting 'domain' param name, parsing a string-literal value into a local 'domain: t.Optional[str] = None', raising TemplateAssertionError on duplicate or non-string-literal. In _make_node() I add a trailing 'domain: t.Optional[str] = None' parameter, and when building the gettext/ngettext call nodes I swap to dgettext/dngettext(domain, ...) when domain is set. I'll also add dgettext/dngettext to _install_callables()' GETTEXT_FUNCTIONS-style globals wiring. No touches to your metadata/note/created/modified logic. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Implementation complete: added note/created/modified metadata support to trans tag. Diff touches: (1) new module const _trans_metadata_keys, (2) parse() while-loop: changed 'if variables:' comma-gate to 'if variables or metadata:', added a new 'if token.value in _trans_metadata_keys: ... continue' branch before the existing assign/trimmed/else chain, (3) _make_node() call site + signature gained trailing 'metadata' param, (4) end of _make_node sets output_node.trans_metadata = metadata only when non-empty. All 830 existing tests pass. Patch written to patch.txt in my sandbox.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Implementation complete: added domain= parameter support to trans tag. Diff touches: (1) GETTEXT_FUNCTIONS gains dgettext/dngettext, (2) new _make_new_dgettext/_make_new_dngettext newstyle wrappers, (3) parse() while-loop: comma-gate changed to 'if variables or domain is not None:', added handling inside the assign branch for token.value == 'domain' (parses string-literal, raises TemplateAssertionError on duplicate or non-literal), (4) _make_node() gained trailing 'domain' param and emits dgettext/dngettext Call nodes instead of gettext/ngettext when domain is set, (5) _install/_install_null/_install_callables wire dgettext/dngettext into environment globals, with null-translations providing domain-agnostic passthroughs. All 830 existing tests pass. Patch written to patch.txt in my sandbox.
